summary: Global Economics Update | 2nd April 2026 The indirect inflation effects of higher energy prices • Beyond their direct effects on consumer prices of fuels and piped gas , higher crude oil and natural gas prices will prompt firms to pass on higher fuel costs to consumers, boosting the prices of non -fuel items of the CPI such as electricity and food .
